topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The accusation landed in the sterile quiet of the administrative office like a dropped stone in still water. I smoothed the crease in my borrowed blazer, a small, almost involuntary gesture of self-soothing. Every word they spoke about the improperly cited passage felt like a dull, predictable echo. I watched the fluorescent light hum overhead, the sound grating against the silence that had settled deep in my chest. There was no fight left in me, just a slow, heavy settling, like dust in an unused room. I nodded once, a small, mechanical movement, and waited for the verdict that I already knew.

[story 2]

I stared at the stack of red-marked papers spread across my desk. The ink seemed to swim before my eyes. When the professor finally laid out the evidence, his voice was level, devoid of anger or surprise. It was just a statement of fact, an unavoidable conclusion. I picked up my pen, not to argue, but just to feel the weight of the plastic casing in my hand. My shoulders slumped forward, a physical manifestation of the effort required to keep standing. I didn't look up when he finished speaking; the truth of the situation had already calcified into something immovable.

[story 3]

The hallway felt endless, the echoing footsteps of other students sounding distant and irrelevant. I walked toward the exit, my pace measured and unhurried. There was no urgency in my steps, just a steady, even rhythm. When the advisor stopped me, offering a final, weary piece of advice, I simply met his gaze with eyes that held no challenge, no spark of defiance. I absorbed the disappointment in his expression, letting it wash over me without absorbing it myself. It was just another administrative hurdle, another expected outcome in a long, monotonous sequence.
